items.docs:
Endpoint to generate a user link token, to be used throughout the vital
link process. The vital link token is a one time use token, that
expires after 10 minutes. If you would like vital-link widget to launch
with a specific provider, pass in a provider in the body. If you would
like to redirect to a custom url after successful or error connection,
pass in your own custom redirect_url parameter.

params.name: expires_at
params.description: When the link code should expire. Defaults to server time plus 1 hour.

items.docs: Generate a token to invite a user of Vital mobile app to your team
This endpoint generates an OAuth link for oauth provider
This connects auth providers that are password based.
This connects auth providers that are password based.
This connects auth providers that are email based.
GET List of all available providers given the generated link token.
POST Connect the given Vital user to a demo provider.

items.docs: POST Create a Vital user given a client_user_id and returns the user_id.

info.name: Get User By Client User Id

params.name: client_user_id
params.description: A unique ID representing the end user. Typically this will be a user ID number from your application. Personally identifiable information, such as an email address or phone number, should not be used in the client_user_id.

params.name: provider
params.description: Provider slug. e.g., `oura`, `fitbit`, `garmin`.

params.name: user_id
params.description: User ID to undo deletion. Mutually exclusive with `client_user_id`.

params.name: client_user_id
params.description: Client User ID to undo deletion. Mutually exclusive with `user_id`.

params.description: Filter by user ID.

params.name: generation_method
params.description: Filter on whether auto-generated lab tests created by Vital, manually created lab tests, or all lab tests should be returned.

params.name: lab_slug
params.description: Filter by the slug of the lab for these lab tests.

params.name: collection_method
params.description: Filter by the collection method for these lab tests.

params.name: status
params.description: Filter by the status of these lab tests.

params.name: marker_ids
params.description: Filter to only include lab tests containing these marker IDs.

params.name: provider_ids
params.description: Filter to only include lab tests containing these provider IDs.

params.name: name
params.description: Filter by the name of the lab test (a case-insensitive substring search).

items.docs: GET all the lab tests the team has access to.

params.description: The slug of the lab for these markers. If both lab_id and lab_slug are provided, lab_slug will be used.

params.description: The name or test code of an individual biomarker or a panel.

params.description: The lab account ID. This lab account is used to determine the availability of markers and lab tests.

items.docs:
Update a modifiable order's scheduled activation date.

The order must be in `ordered` or `awaiting_registration` status. Setting
`activate_by` to a future date reschedules dispatch; setting it to `null`
clears the schedule and enqueues immediate dispatch for `ordered` orders.

Returns 400 when:
- the order is not in a modifiable status,
- the order was created for immediate processing (cannot be scheduled
  after the fact),
- `activate_by` is in the past.

params.name: order_id

Creates a parse job, uploads the file(s) to provider, persists the job row,
and starts the ParseLabReport. Returns a generated job_id.

Retrieves the parse job status and stored result if completed.

Returns:
    ParseLabResultJobResponse with job status and parsed data (if complete)
